serinfo.sername: US Geological Survey Scientific Investigations Map

descript.abstract: Digital compilation and reinterpretation of published and unpublished geologic mapping of Alaska.
descript.purpose: This data set represents part of a systematic effort to release geologic map data for the United States in a uniform manner. Geologic data in this data set has been compiled from a wide variety of sources, published and unpublished, ranging from state and regional geologic map to field mapping.

idinfo.useconst: None. Users are advised to read the data set's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations. Acknowledgment of the U.S. Geological Survey would be appreciated in products derived from these data.

cntorgp.cntorg: U.S. Geological Survey, Alaska Science Center

idinfo.native: Environment as of Metadata Creation: Microsoft Windows 7 Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; ESRI ArcCatalog 10.2 (Build 3552) Service Pack [N/A] (Build [N/A])

attracc.attraccr: Many types of source materials of varying scales were used to compile this data set. Sources include previously published maps, unpublished mapping, and air photo interpretation, and reflects more than a century of work in Alaska. Vector features were mostly digitized either by digitizing on screen or on a large format digitizing tablet. Vectors and polygons were attributed and checked. Topology rules were checked.

dataqual.logic: The geologic data consists of attributed arcs and polygons. Each polygon has a coded attribute designating the mapped geologic unit. Faults, stratigraphic contacts, dikes, lineations and other linear features are represented as arcs. When a fault is also a stratigraphic contact, only a single arc is present. Dangling arcs may be present (fault, dike, lineation, etc.).
dataqual.complete: The data set AKStategeol.gdb consists of arc, polygon, and point features depicting the geology of Alaska in both detailed and generalized versions. The detailed state map geologic units are described in the Description of Map Units (DMU) found in the accompanying pamphlet. The features for the generalized version were derived from the detailed features; the generalized version is used for the printed version of the state map. A separate metadata file is provided for the generalized version. The AKStategeol.gdb also includes many tables which contain additional information and can be linked to either the arc or polygon features. The information in the tables were derived from a set of related Filemaker databases which contain additional information. The native Filemaker database files and a stand-alone Filemaker_Runtime file, along with CSV and DBF files are provided with this publication. The features in AKStategeol.gdb are also provided as shapefiles.

horizpa.horizpar: Data is presented for use at a nominal scale of 1:250,000.

procstep.procdesc: The geology for each of the 153 1:250,000 scale quadrangles in Alaska was compiled from many different sources. Data capture has been ongoing since 1995. Linework was hand digitized from materials of various media and scale. The arcs and polygons were attributed, and topology was checked. Data is archived as ARC/Info coverages.
The geology data from the 153 quadrangle coverages were combined to create the detailed map feature classes in AKStatewidegeol.gdb

mapproj.mapprojn: NAD 1983 Alaska Albers (ESRI Full Name: NAD_1983_Alaska_Albers)

enttyp.enttypd: Characteristics of the geologic unit are coded in the polygon attribute table. The table includes the following items: CLASS, QCLASS, SOURCE, NSACLASS, NSASUB, NSAMOD, SOURCECLASS, STATE_SYMBOL, STATE_LABEL, STATE_LABEL2, STATE_UNITNAME, AGE_RANGE, and SEQUENCE.

attr.attrdef: Unique numeric code assigned to a geologic unit from an original source map. Number is unique only within a given quadrangle and specific to SOURCE.

codesetd.codesetn: nsaunits (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Subdivision of attribute NSACLASS providing more detailed Quaternary classification. Field QCLASS is a key field for linking to field QCLASS in table nsaqkey.

codesetd.codesetn: nsaunits (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Unique code assigned to each source; uses the 2-letter quadrangle code and a three digit number. By default, 001 is reserved for the topographic map or other source of hydrologic spatial data for each quadrangle. Field SOURCE is a key field and links to SOURCE in table nsarefs.

codesetd.codesetn: nsarefs (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Regional numeric code assigned to similar geologic units. Field NSACLASS is a key field and links to field NSACLASS in tables nsaage, nsakey, nsalith, nsarefs, and nsasetting. NSACLASS is also a 1:many relationship with NSACLASS in table nsaunits.

codesetd.codesetn: nsakey (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Same coding as NSACLASS, but used to define the bedrock unit beneath surficial deposits. Not universally populated.

codesetd.codesetn: nsakey (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Indicates if a geologic unit is altered, contact metamorphosed, or has a queried unit assignment. Some maps show contact metamorphosed areas as separate units; these units are assigned a NSACLASS for the appropriate protolith and have 'HFS' selected as NSAMOD value. If only a few polygons of a geologic unit are altered or contact metamorphosed, then the NSAMOD value is set for those polygons only. Queried units, Tk? versus Tk for example, have the same NSACLASS, but Tk? will have NSAMOD = 'Q'.

edom.edomvd: Unit is not altered, contact metamorphosed, or queried.

attr.attrdef: Concatenated field that uniquely identifies a given source map (SOURCE) and geologic unit (CLASS). Field SOURCECLASS is a key field that links to field SOURCE_CLASS in table nsaunits.

codesetd.codesetn: nsaunits (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Number defining symbol color in WPGCMYKG.style for ArcGIS to display the detailed digital geologic map in AKStategeol.mxd.
Same as STATE_LABEL2 but uses special characters for using FGDCGeoAge font to display the symbols for geologic time periods.

attrdomv.udom: } represents Mesozoic; ^ Triassic; | Paleozoic; * Pennsylvanian; _ Cambrian; = preCambrian; and < Proterozoic.

attr.attrdef: Geologic unit label used in the Description of Map Units and detailed version of the state map. Label is in standard text font and does not use special characters. Px is used for Proterozoic and IP for Pennsylvanian.

codesetd.codesetn: gen_state_link (provided as Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Unit name as used in the Description of Map Units

codesetd.codesetn: gen_state_link (provided as Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Age range of the map unit in the Description of Map Units, does not necessarily match the units on the printed generalized map. The combining of DMU units for the generalized map may have created longer time intervals for the units.

attrdomv.udom: Describes the geologic age interval of the geologic unit as used in the Description of Map Units (DMU). Information obtained from gen_state_link (provided as Filemaker databse, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: A sorting code using a letter for lithologic type and then a number. Used to place the unit in age order. Matches the order given in the Description of Map Units.

enttyp.enttypd: Characteristics of linear features, which include various types of faults, stratigraphic contacts, and other line types. Lineations and dikes are stored in separate feature classes, but contain similar attributes. Items in AKStategeol_arc include the following items: ARC_CODE, ARC_PARA1, SOURCE, LINEID, LINE_TYPE, LEFT_LABEL, and RIGHT_LABEL.

attr.attrdef: ARC_CODE is a positive integer value in the arc attribute table. Values range discontinuously from 0 to 99. Values match one-for-one with LINE_TYPE.

edom.edomvd: Normal fault; location certain; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location approximate; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location inferred, queried; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Shoreline or riverbank; arc-para1 = 1 designates single-sided stream, typically used in areas of braiding
Internal contact or phase change in the source map.
Boundary of altered zone or hornfels
Thrust fault; location certain; teeth on right from origin. ARC_PARA1 is dip of the fault plane, if known. A negative number indicates overturned fault.
Thrust fault; location approximate; teeth on right from origin. ARC_PARA1 is dip of the fault plane, if known. A negative number indicates overturned fault.
Thrust fault; location inferred, queried; teeth on right from origin
Moraine or till margin (scour) on bedrock
Moraine limit having tics on right side from origin. ARC_PARA1 = 1 indicates the moraine is submerged.
Caldera or crater rim, inferred or concealed
Fault with unknown offset; location certain
Fault with unknown offset; location approximate
Fault with unknown offset; location inferred
Terrace, erosional, or wave-cut scarp; digitize with water on right from origin
High-angle reverse fault; certain location; teeth on right from origin; arc-para1 is angle of thrusting, if known
High-angle reverse fault; certain approximate; teeth on right from origin; arc-para1 is angle of thrusting, if known
High-angle reverse fault; certain inferred; teeth on right from origin; arc-para1 is angle of thrusting, if known
Dikes and sills (used in feature class AKStategeol_dike)
Concealed normal fault; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Concealed thrust fault; teeth on right from origin. ARC_PARA1 is dip of the fault plane, if known. A negative number indicates overturned fault.
Concealed normal fault having right lateral oblique slip
Concealed normal fault having left lateral oblique slip
Normal fault; location certain; having right lateral oblique slip; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location approximate; having right lateral oblique slip; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location certain; having left lateral oblique slip; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location approximate; having left lateral oblique slip; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location inferred or queried; having left lateral oblique slip; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Normal fault; location inferred or queried; having right lateral oblique slip; if arc-para1 = 1, then upthrown side is on right-hand side
Right lateral fault; location inferred, queried
Left lateral fault; location inferred, queried
Scratch line used where polygon on source map was not closed, or limit of mapping
Bounding neat line of 1:250,000 scale quadrangle

attr.attrdef: Used as a modifier for ARC_CODE

attrdomv.udom: See ARC_CODE attribute definitions for ARC_PARA1 definition

attr.attrdef: Unique code assigned to each source; uses the 2-letter quadrangle code and a three digit number. By default, 001 is reserved for the topographic map or other source of hydrologic spatial data for each quadrangle. Field SOURCE is a key field for linking to field SOURCE in table nsarefs.

codesetd.codesetn: nsarefs (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: LINEID provides additional attributes for some faults and glacial moraines. For faults, LINEID identifies the fault system the feature belongs to. For moraines, LINEID identifies the moraine or glacial limit. LINEID is an 8 character alpha-numeric code following this naming convention. The first letter is 'f' if the feature is a fault, or 'm' if a moraine. The second and third characters are alpha abbreviations for the fault system, region, or glaciation. These abbreviations are defined below. The final 4 numbers in LINEID are identifiers which further subdivide the line's description. Field LINEID is a key field for linking to field LINEID in table LINEID.

edom.edomvd: faults in the Chugach Mountains region
Laurentia region, Kandik River area of Alaska
faults in the Yakutat block region, south of the Fairweather-Chugach-Ragged Mountain fault system
used for symbolizing small scale maps; identifies faults not to be drawn in areas of high density
same as 'hide' but feature to be drawn as ARC_CODE = 2 if map is to show contacts.

attr.attrdef: Geologic unit on the left side of the line feature.
Geologic unit on the right side of the line feature.
Descriptive name for ARC_CODE

enttyp.enttypd: Line features representing dikes and sills

attr.attrdef: same as attribute ARC_CODE in AKStategeol_arc feature class
Unique code assigned to each source; uses the 2-letter quadrangle code and a three digit number. Same as attribute SOURCE in AKStategeol_arc and AKStategeol_poly.

codesetd.codesetn: nsarefs (available as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Unique numeric code assigned to a geologic unit from the original source map. Number is unique only within a given quadrangle and specific to SOURCE. Not universally populated.

codesetd.codesetn: nsaunits (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Regional numeric code assigned to similar units. Not universally populated.

codesetd.codesetn: nsakey (provided as a geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Descriptive name for ARC_CODE
same as attribute ARC_CODE in AKStategeol_arc feature class
Unique code assigned to each source; uses the 2-letter quadrangle code and a three digit number. Same as attribute SOURCE in AKStategeol_arc and AKStategeol_poly.

codesetd.codesetn: nsarefs (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Descriptive name for ARC_CODE

enttyp.enttypd: lineid is a table in AKStategeol.gdb containing additional information about some faults and moraines.

attr.attrdef: LINEID provides additional attributes for some faults and moraines. LINEID is the keyfield for linking to AKStategeol_arc.

attrdomv.udom: Describes fault system or region this feature is found. See description of LINEID for AKStategeol_arc.

attr.attrdef: Descriptive name of fault system, region, or moraine

attrdomv.udom: Descriptive name of fault system, region, or moraine

enttyp.enttypd: Table containing geologic age information for each unique NSACLASS value. Provides composite maximum and minimum age assignments for a given NSACLASS. A modified version of International Union of Geological Sciences (IUGS) International Chronostratigraphic Chart or radiometric dating were used to determine age attributes.

attr.attrdef: key field to link to AKStategeol_poly

codesetd.codesetn: nsakey (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Minimum age in millions of years, determined either from IUGS or radiometric dating.
Maximum age in millions of years, determined either from IUGS or radiometric dating.
Defines whether the age assignment is relative, if based on stratigraphic position, or absolute, if based on radiometric dating. The default value is 'Relative'.

edom.edomvd: Age assignment is based on radiometric dating
Age assignment is based on stratigraphic position. Default value.

attr.attrdef: Age_comments
The minimum or youngest age assignment for the eon of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The minimum or youngest age assignment for the era of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The minimum or youngest age assignment for the period of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The minimum or youngest age assignment for the epoch of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The minimum or youngest age assignment for the age of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The maximum or oldest age assignment for the eon of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The maximum or oldest age assignment for the era of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The maximum or oldest age assignment for the period of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The maximum or oldest age assignment for the epoch of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
The maximum or oldest age assignment for the age of the unit, based on geologic interpretation.
Concatenation of fields MIN_EON, MIN_ERA, MIN_PERIOD, MIN_EPOCH, and MIN_AGE.
The most precise minimum age coded; derived from FULL_MIN field.
Concatenation of fields MAX_EON, MAX_ERA, MAX_PERIOD, MAX_EPOCH, and MAX_AGE.
The most precise maximum age coded; derived from FULL_MAX field.

enttyp.enttypd: Table containing labels and colors applied to the map. The table also includes a brief description of the geologic unit. Key field is NSACLASS.

attr.attrdef: unique numeric identifier
Symbol number for wpgcmykg.style; used for symbolizing polygons for the detailed state geologic map; based on NSACLASS
Regional numeric code assigned to similar units.
Map labels used for regional maps, which may be different from the generalized and/or detailed state maps.
Brief summary of the regional geologic unit, which may be different from the generalized and/or detailed state maps.

enttyp.enttypd: Table containing the lithologic coding for each regional geologic unit. Geologic units are comprised of more than one rock type, therefore lithology to NSACLASS is a many-to-one relationship. Each lithology is described in a 5 tiered hierarchy and is given a rank based on abundance.

attr.attrdef: unique numeric identifier
Regional numeric code assigned to similar geologic units. Key field to link to AKStategeol_poly.
Highest level lithologic classification. Text field.
Second level lithologic classification. LITH2 is based on value of LITH1. Text field.
Third level lithologic classification. LITH3 is based on value of LITH2. Text field.
Fourth level lithologic classification. LITH4 is based on value of LITH3. Text field.
Fifth level lithologic classification. LITH5 is based on value of LITH4. Text field.
Concatenation of LITH1, LITH2, LITH3, LITH4, and LITH5. This field allows for searches on any level of the lithologic hierarchy.
Copy of the lith field with the most specific part of the lithologic assignment.
Description of lithologic form.
Indicates how much of the geologic map unit this lithology represents.

edom.edomvd: Greater than or equal to 33 percent

Abundance of this lithology is not defined. The term 'major' is part of the attribute to insure inclusive searches for major rock types, as rock types listed in the indeterminate category could be a major component of a geologic map unit. These may be eliminated from search results by explicitly omitting 'Indeterminate' from the query results.

attr.attrdef: Optional field containing an estimate of percentage of the unit that this lithology represents. This information is rarely available in Alaska.
comment field

enttyp.enttypd: Similar to NSAKEY except it subdivides the Quaternary surficial deposits by assigning color symbols and labels to the Quaternary units.

attr.attrdef: Symbol number for wpgcmykg.style; used for symbolizing polygons based on Quaternary units, QCLASS
Subdivision of attribute NSACLASS providing more detailed Quaternary units. Key field linking to AKStategeol_poly.

codesetd.codesetn: nsaunits (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Regional numeric code assigned to similar geologic units.

codesetd.codesetn: nsakey (provided as geodatabase table, Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and CSV and DBF files)

attr.attrdef: Geologic unit label for statewide Quaternary map
Brief description of the surficial map unit

enttyp.enttypd: Reference citation for source map and/or other publication used to build AKStategeol data set.

attr.attrdef: unique numeric identifier

Unique code assigned to each source; uses the 2-letter 1:250,000 scale quadrangle code and a three digit number. By default, 001 is reserved for the topographic map or other source of hydrologic spatial data for each quadrangle. If a source map extends into multiple quadrangles, it has been assigned a source code for each quadrangle covered.

attrdomv.udom: Unique code assigned to each source that uses the two letter 1:250,000 scale quadrangle code and a three digit number.

attr.attrdef: Reference citation

attrdomv.udom: Reference citation written in USGS style.

enttyp.enttypd: Geologic setting for each unique NSACLASS value

attr.attrdef: Regional numeric code assigned to similar geologic units.
Description of geologic setting for each unique NSACLASS.

enttyp.enttypd: Table derived from the central database, NSAUNITS. Contains brief abstracts of the unit descriptions from each SOURCE map, which have been classified into regional and statewide units.

attr.attrdef: Name of 1:250,000 scale quadrangle. The name of the Mount McKinley 1:250,000 scale quadrangle was changed to Denali in September 2015. The records for this area are duplicated as both Mount McKinley(MM) and Denali(DN).
Geologic unit label from the SOURCE map.
Map unit name from the SOURCE map.
Geologic age of the unit as given on the SOURCE map.
Abstracted version of the unit description from the SOURCE map. Focus on lithology and important relationships as described on the SOURCE map.
General classification of geologic unit. For mixed units, the dominant category is given.

attrdomv.udom: Igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic, unconsolidated, or melange. For mixed units, the dominant category is given.

attr.attrdef: Brief notes on any fossil control mentioned on the SOURCE map.
Brief notes on radiometric ages mentioned on the SOURCE map.

Unique code assigned to each source; used the 2-letter code for 1:250,000 scale quadrangles, and a three digit number. By default, 001 is used for the topographic map or other source of hydrologic spatial data for each quadrangle. Numbers above 100 indicate sources that were not captured digitally.
Unique numeric code assigned to each geologic unit on a SOURCE map. Unique only within a given quadrangle and specific to each SOURCE.
Concatenation of fields SOURCE and CLASS.
Regional numeric code assigned to like geologic units. Link to many of the other databases or tables.
Indicates if geologic unit is altered, contact metamorphosed, or has a queried unit assignment.
Regional numeric code assigned to similar surficial units.
Used to define the bedrock unit (NSACLASS) underlying surficial deposits. Not universally populated.

enttyp.enttypd: Point feature class with radiometric age data for samples analyzed by K/Ar, 40Ar/39Ar, Rb/Sr, fission-track, and other methods, excluding U/Pb determinations. Created from Radiometric database, which is provided as a Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and DBF and CSV files.

attr.attrdef: Unique numeric identifier for this data set
Sample number/identifier
Name of 1:250,000 scale quadrangle
Latitude given in decimal degrees, NAD27
Longitude given in decimal degrees, NAD27
Rock type of sample dated
Mineral or phase dated
Dating method used
Comments about the sample or analysis.
Reference citations for the age determination
Reported age in millions of years
Analytical error for the age calculation, if reported
40Ar/39Ar plateau age, if reported
Analytical error for the 40Ar/39Ar plateau age, if reported
40Ar/39Ar isochron age, if reported
Analytical error for the 40Ar/39Ar isochron age, if reported
40Ar/39Ar total fusion age, if reported
Analytical error for the 40Ar/39Ar total fusion age, if reported

enttyp.enttypd: Point feature class of U-Pb age data for samples analyzed by uranium-lead methods. Created from u-pb database, which is provided as a Filemaker database, Filemaker runtime, and DBF and CSV files.

attr.attrdef: unique numeric identifier for this data table
Sample number/identifier
Name of 1:250,000 scale quadrangle
Latitude given in decimal degrees, NAD27
Longitude given in decimal degrees, NAD27
Rock type of sample dated
Mineral or phase dated
Dating method used

edom.edomvd: Thermal ionization mass spectrometry single grain

attr.attrdef: Comments about the sample or analysis.
Reference citations for the age determination
Interpreted best age based on the data; reported in millions of years
Analytical error for the preferred age, if reported
Number of standard deviations of reported analytical error
Additional description of the interpreted age or sample.
Additional description of the interpreted age.
206Pb/238U age
206Pb/238U age error
207Pb/235U age
207Pb/235U age error
207Pb/206Pb age
207Pb/206Pb age error
208Pb/232Th age
208Pb/232Th age error
206Pb/204Pb age
206Pb/204Pb age error
Upper concordia intercept
Upper concordia intercept error
Lower concordia intercept
Lower concordia intercept error

distinfo.distliab: This publication was prepared by an agency of the United States Government. Neither the United States Government nor any agency thereof, nor any of their employees, makes any warranty, expressed or implied, or assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, product, or process disclosed in this report, or represents that its use would not infringe privately owned rights. Reference therein to any specific commercial product, process, or service by trade name, trademark, manufacturer, or otherwise does not necessarily constitute or imply its endorsement, recommendation, or favoring by the United States Government or any agency thereof.

stdorder.fees: None. No fees are applicable for obtaining the data set.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
